clinical_interpretation: High value usually means rhythm irregularity; often important for arrhythmia separation.
Captures beat spacing; strongly linked to rhythm timing.
Useful vital sign, but average BPM alone cannot fully diagnose arrhythmia.
Often redundant with heart rate because both measure beat frequency.
Captures amplitude variability and signal strength.
Measures signal power; may reflect waveform amplitude and recording characteristics.
Captures sharp peaks or heavy tails in the waveform.
